title: Depth-First Search, Recursion, Paths, and Backtracking · Behavior Case: Dfs Contract
summary: DFS marks on return, confuses visitation with solution path, fails to undo dead ends, and promises shortest routes. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dfs contract increment in traceable DFS solver with path witness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define start, goal, passable-neighbor order, visited policy, result, and failure contract before traversal.
Select a generator from player, performance, reproducibility, topology, and accessibility goals with explicit non-guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-dfs-solver-recursion-5
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-dfs-solver-recursion-5.
# Case 9036: a low-vision player session.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee.
def worked_maze_dfs_solver_recursion_5(path_steps: int = 31, valid_steps: int = 31, explored: int = 42, repeated_vertices: int = 0, recursion_safe: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "gridworked5",
        "topology9036",
        "search5dekrow",
        "eventworked59036",
        "access9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if path_steps < 1 or valid_steps < 0 or explored < path_steps:
        return False, valid_steps, "dfs-count"
    if valid_steps != path_steps:
        return False, valid_steps, "invalid-path-step"
    if repeated_vertices > 0:
        return False, repeated_vertices, "visited-policy"
    if not recursion_safe:
        return False, explored, "recursion-limit"
    assert valid_steps == path_steps
    return True, path_steps, "valid-dfs-route"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define start, goal, passable-neighbor order, visited policy, result, and failure contract before traversal.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Dfs Contract model · Theory Dfs Contract, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Dfs Contract precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Dfs Contract model · Theory Dfs Contract, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 6821 during a classroom algorithm comparison. Build deterministic Python evidence to validate DFS visitation, backtracking, route witness, termination, and depth limits; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then switch the solver or generator and restate each guarantee. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
